summary: The sprint code is generally well-structured and follows project conventions. The new files are appropriately sized and co-located. The primary maintainability concerns are: (1) the fx-gain-loss widget in AnalysisSection.tsx is architecturally misleading — it fetches user-wide data in a portfolio-scoped component without any indication in the UI that the data covers all portfolios, which will cause confusion as the codebase grows; (2) the duplicate PortfolioHistoryPoint interface definition appears in both dashboard/page.tsx and AnalysisSection.tsx; (3) the hardcoded 'NAS' exchange code for all non-domestic overseas holdings is a silent assumption that will fail for ETFs or non-NASDAQ US securities; (4) the tasks.md follow-up items for RD-4, RD-7, RD-8, RD-9 represent real functional gaps that will need explicit tracking to not become permanent tech debt.

findings.title: FX gain/loss widget in AnalysisSection is user-scoped, not portfolio-scoped — misleading in context
findings.detail: AnalysisSection receives portfolioId as a prop and is rendered inside a portfolio detail page. The portfolio-history and benchmark-delta queries are correctly portfolio-scoped (portfolio_id param). However, the fx-gain-loss query has no portfolioId parameter — it calls /analytics/fx-gain-loss which returns user-wide FX data across all portfolios. The UI comment '// FX gain/loss — user-wide (해외주식)' acknowledges this but the card has no label clarifying the scope difference. A user with 2 portfolios (one domestic, one overseas) will see the same fx-gain-loss numbers on both portfolio pages, creating false attribution. This is a data integrity / UX correctness concern dressed as a maintainability issue.
findings.fix: Either (a) pass portfolioId to the fx-gain-loss API endpoint and filter server-side, or (b) add a visible UI label 'across all portfolios' to the card header so users understand the scope, or (c) remove the widget from the portfolio-scoped view and keep it only in the global analytics page. Option (b) is the minimum fix; option (a) is the correct fix.

findings.title: PortfolioHistoryPoint interface duplicated across two files
findings.detail: The identical interface `PortfolioHistoryPoint { date: string; value: number }` is defined in both files. Similarly, BenchmarkDelta is defined in both dashboard/page.tsx (line 116) and AnalysisSection.tsx (line 88). As more pages consume analytics data, this will proliferate.
findings.fix: Extract shared analytics types to frontend/src/types/analytics.ts (or a co-located types file). Import in both files. This is a low-priority cleanup but worth doing before a third consumer appears.

findings.title: Overseas dividend collection hardcodes 'NAS' exchange for all non-domestic tickers
findings.detail: In collect_dividends_for_user_holdings, all non-domestic tickers (those that aren't 6-digit Korean codes) are fetched using `_fetch_overseas_dividend(ticker, 'NAS', ...)` — hardcoding NASDAQ as the exchange. A holding on NYSE (AAPL → fine, but XOM is NYSE), AMEX, or LSE would be queried with the wrong EXCD parameter, likely returning empty results or incorrect data. The Holding model presumably stores exchange/market info but it's not used here.
findings.fix: Join Holding with its market/exchange_code field in the query at line 413, and pass the correct market code to _fetch_overseas_dividend. If the Holding model lacks an exchange field, add one. As a stopgap, log a warning when falling back to NAS.

findings.title: OrderDialogProvider picks first portfolio without any indication — silent UX assumption
findings.detail: The component selects portfolios[0].id as the target portfolio and the JSDoc comment acknowledges this is a known limitation. For a user with multiple portfolios (portfolio A = Korean stocks, portfolio B = US stocks), clicking Buy on a US stock will always try to place the order into portfolio A. There is no UI cue, no selection prompt. This is already tracked as a follow-up in tasks.md (RD-4) but needs a severity assignment for prioritization.
findings.fix: This is a P2 follow-up. Minimum viable improvement: if the ticker exists in only one portfolio, auto-select that one. If it exists in multiple or none, show a portfolio selector inside the OrderDialog before showing the buy/sell form. Track as a dedicated issue with priority 2.

findings.title: design-preview spec uses page.waitForTimeout — flaky test pattern
findings.detail: page.waitForTimeout(800) is used to 'let chart animations settle'. This is a Playwright anti-pattern: fixed timeouts cause flakiness on slow CI machines and mask non-deterministic rendering. The test already disables animations via `animations: 'disabled'` in toHaveScreenshot, making the timeout redundant.
findings.fix: Replace waitForTimeout(800) with a condition-based wait: await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle') or await expect(page.locator('[data-testid=chart]')).toBeVisible(). Remove the timeout entirely given animations are disabled.
